report_name: Novel AI 'Feedback Loop' Attack Triggers 4-Hour Market Freeze at Financial Hub

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Attackers
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated threat actor who executed a novel 'feedback loop' attack against an AI-driven SOAR platform by generating millions of low-grade security alerts to trigger a defensive response that caused a denial of service.
threat_actors.goals: To cause operational and financial disruption to a major financial institution by weaponizing its automated defense systems.

attack_patterns.name: Denial of AI Service
attack_patterns.description: Attackers generated a massive volume of fake, low-grade security alerts to overwhelm an AI-driven SOAR platform, causing it to initiate a self-inflicted denial of service by quarantining the trading floor network.

attack_patterns.name: Poison Training Data
attack_patterns.description: The attack involved a form of data poisoning or flooding, where millions of fabricated security events were fed into the SOAR platform to manipulate its decision-making model and trigger an unintended, catastrophic response.

attack_patterns.name: Endpoint Denial of Service
attack_patterns.description: The AI-driven SOAR platform's automated response resulted in an endpoint denial of service for the entire trading floor, as the network segment was quarantined, preventing systems from operating.

attack_patterns.name: Network Denial of Service
attack_patterns.description: The trading floor network was effectively taken offline by the SOAR platform's quarantine action, which was triggered by the attackers' flood of fake alerts.

vulnerabilities.name: Logical Vulnerability in AI-driven SOAR Platform
vulnerabilities.description: A logical vulnerability in the AI's decision-making model was exploited. The model lacked a mechanism to distinguish a high-volume flood of trivial events from a genuine, sophisticated attack, leading it to trigger an extreme and unwarranted containment protocol.

identities.name: Unnamed Global Financial Hub
identities.description: A major global financial institution whose AI-powered SOAR platform was targeted, resulting in a four-hour market freeze.

infrastructure.name: Alert Generation Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A network of systems used by the attackers to generate and send millions of fake, low-grade security alerts from diverse IPs to the target's SOAR platform.

indicators.name: Anomalous SOAR Alert Volume Spike
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous SOAR or SIEM Alert Volume Spike
id: 2b1a987c-5432-4f1e-8d9a-0c7b6a5e4d3c
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects a massive spike in the volume of incoming alerts to a SOAR or SIEM platform. This could indicate a 'feedback loop' or flooding attack designed to manipulate automated defenses, as described in the report of an attack on a financial hub.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEgf1yUeXCo5kjDkE_6xDJTVTJ0a5oRApwTg86x3WlhyXTW6CYo4DPzvCvNCdXQOjT_I61h85fv2VU4L3ECv3aYGec9CNU8RscfWm9YVwiaxgMm2azL8sS8DiApoYoZfG-ytuNia6M=
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-05
tags:
  - attack.aml.t0029
  - attack.denial_of_service
level: high
logsource:
  category: application
  product: siem
detection:
  # This is a conceptual rule. Actual implementation would require aggregation and thresholding capabilities in the SIEM.
  # Example: count(events) > 10000 in 1 minute
  keywords:
    - 'A massive number of alerts were generated in a short period of time, exceeding normal operational thresholds by several orders of magnitude.'
  condition: keywords
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate large-scale security incidents or system misconfigurations.
indicators.description: Detects a massive spike in the volume of alerts being ingested by a SIEM or SOAR platform, which could be indicative of an AI feedback loop attack.

indicators.name: High Volume of Diverse Low-Grade Security Events
indicators.pattern:
title: High Volume of Diverse Low-Grade Security Events
id: 8e4d3c2b-1a98-4f1e-8d9a-0c7b6a5e4d3c
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects a high volume of low-severity security events originating from an unusually large number of distinct source IPs. This pattern matches the 'noise generation' phase of the feedback loop attack described in the report.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEgf1yUeXCo5kjDkE_6xDJTVTJ0a5oRApwTg86x3WlhyXTW6CYo4DPzvCvNCdXQOjT_I61h85fv2VU4L3ECv3aYGec9CNU8RscfWm9YVwiaxgMm2azL8sS8DiApoYoZfG-ytuNia6M=
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-05
tags:
  - attack.aml.t0020
  - attack.denial_of_service
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  # This is a conceptual rule. Actual implementation would require stateful correlation (e.g., distinct count of source IPs over a time window).
  # Example: distinct_count(src_ip) > 1000 AND severity = 'low' over 5 minutes.
  keywords:
    - 'A high number of low-severity alerts from a wide range of source IPs was observed, consistent with a noise generation attack.'
  condition: keywords
falsepositives:
  - Large-scale scanning activity from legitimate research projects.
  - Distributed brute-force attempts.
indicators.description: Detects a flood of low-grade security events (e.g., failed logins) from a large number of different source IPs, matching the pattern of the feedback loop attack.

relationships.target_name: Logical Vulnerability in AI-driven SOAR Platform

relationships.source_name: High Volume of Diverse Low-Grade Security Events
